title: Research data supporting 'EEG recording during playing MOBA game'

funding_references.funder_name: This work was supported by a grant from the Chongqing Medical Youth Top Talent Project in Chongqing Municipal Health Commission (DDZ).

funding_references.award_title: Chongqing Medical Youth Top Talent Project

description: This dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG) recordings from 23 participants during resting state (eyes closed and open) and active gameplay in a multiplayer online battle arena (MOBA) game. EEG signals were acquired using a 64-electrode Neuroscan system at 1000 Hz sampling rate, with event markers corresponding to in-game actions (kills, deaths, game start, failure, and victory). The dataset supports investigation of neural correlates of competitive gaming and real-time cognitive engagement.
methods_description: EEG data were collected using a Neuroscan system with 64 electrodes at a sampling frequency of 1000 Hz. Recordings included resting-state conditions (eyes closed and eyes open) and continuous EEG during MOBA gameplay. Event markers were coded to identify specific in-game events: kills (13), deaths (14), game start (66), game failure (444), and game victory (666). Data collection occurred between April 2023 and July 2023.
